title: tithymalus peplus subsp. maritimus
intro: Tithymalus peplus subsp. maritimus, also known as sea milk thistle or prickly milk thistle, is a subspecies of the Tithymalus peplus plant, which is a succulent perennial native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have shown potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and wound-healing properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Tithymalus peplus subsp. maritimus can be prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
